nativeRange.habitat: Banks of small forest streams; grows attached to rocks or rooted in mud at the water's edge, often partially emergent.

decorPreferences: porous rock (lava, dragon stone) for attachment
open vertical space for tall arrowhead leaves

summary: The tallest commonly traded Anubias, with distinctive spear-tip arrowhead leaves on long stalks. Generally regarded as an easy, slow-growing epiphyte best suited to background placement attached to driftwood or rock.
careNotes: Anubias hastifolia is generally considered the largest Anubias species in the trade, often reaching 30-50 cm or more in established tanks — significantly taller than A. barteri or A. nana. The spear-tip / arrowhead leaves grow on long stalks, making it a strong background specimen. As with all Anubias, it is an epiphyte: the rhizome should be attached to driftwood or rock (super glue gel, fishing line, or wedging) and kept ABOVE the substrate, otherwise the rhizome tends to rot. Tolerates a wide light range but is slow-growing and is commonly reported to accumulate algae under high light; low-to-medium light is the safer default. CO2 is optional but improves growth and reduces algae pressure. Generally advised to keep around 22-26°C with stable, well-cycled water — sudden parameter swings have been reported to cause leaf melt. Liquid fertilization (especially iron and trace elements) is helpful since the plant draws nutrients from the water column rather than the substrate. Native to West and Central African forest streams (Ghana through Angola). Cross-source notes: Tropica primary page did not render via direct fetch (server-side XSLT error during access); husbandry parameters were instead cross-checked against Aquarium Co-Op (assigned secondary), Buce Plant, Fish Laboratory, and Splashy Fish — all four agree on the temperature/pH/lighting/placement profile reported here.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Tropica's plant-detail pages returned an XSLT/MacroEngine server error during direct WebFetch on the access date; data attributed to Tropica was confirmed via Tropica-referenced summaries surfaced through web search. additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: Confirmed slow growth, low-light tolerance, epiphyte (super-glue-to-hardscape) habit, background placement, and tall-Anubias size class.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Cross-checked size (15-40 cm / 6-16 in), pH 6.0-7.5, easy care level, rhizome propagation, and the warning that unstable parameters cause melt/rot.

additional.name: Fish Laboratory
additional.notes: Cross-checked family (Araceae), temperature 22-28°C, pH 6.0-7.5, slow growth (~1 leaf/month), and propagation via rhizome division.

additional.name: Kew Plants of the World Online
additional.notes: Authoritative native-range reference: West Tropical Africa to Angola (Ghana, Nigeria, Cameroon, Gabon, DRC, Angola).
